name: Numba (JIT Compiler for Python)

performance.notes: JIT compilation adds startup overhead (seconds); compiled code runs at C/FORTRAN speed

agent_readiness.notes: JIT compiler for Python and NumPy code via LLVM. Decorates Python functions with @jit, @njit, @vectorize, @cuda.jit. Compiles numeric Python (numpy, math operations) to machine code. GPU support via CUDA kernels (@cuda.jit). Parallel loops via prange(). Often achieves C/FORTRAN performance. No C extensions needed. Used by scientific computing, simulation, and ML numerical code. Numba-compiled functions work seamlessly with NumPy arrays.

gotchas: First call compiles (cold start latency) — warm up in production
Not all Python code compilable — works best with numeric loops/arrays
Complex Python objects (classes, dicts) require OBJECT mode (slow)
CUDA support requires NVIDIA GPU + CUDA toolkit
Debugging compiled code harder than Python
best_when: Numeric Python loops that NumPy vectorization can't handle
Scientific simulations needing C speed from Python code
Custom CUDA kernels without writing C/CUDA directly
Optimizing tight numerical loops in data processing
avoid_when: Non-numeric code (string processing, I/O, business logic)
One-off scripts (JIT overhead not worth it)
When NumPy vectorization already handles the problem
summary: Numba is a JIT compiler for Python that turns numeric Python functions into C/FORTRAN-speed machine code via LLVM. Simple @jit/@njit decorators. CUDA GPU kernel support. Parallel loops via prange(). Best for numeric Python loops that NumPy vectorization can't handle. No C extensions needed — pure Python syntax, compiled performance. Essential for scientific computing and numerical simulation.
